rulings.Case Title: IN RE THE JASMINE LIN IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/29/20 for an amendment to be filed: paragraph 16 of the petition is not supported by Exhibit D and does not agree with the prayer paragraph 5, and Exhibit D is missing the timesheets. [CRC 7.751(b).] Additionally, in interim accounts, estimated fees will not be allowed; actual time spent must be requested in the subequesnt account petition. Although Trustee fees requested this time appear to be more reasonable, the time sheets attached as Exhibit C to the fee declaration filed on 5/27/20 are entirely inadequate. [See reference to the CRC above.] The Notice of Hearing should indicate in item 1 the representative capacity (here, Trustee) of the party who has filed the petition that is to be heard. A courtesy copy of the amendment and the proof of service of the amendment must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/18/20.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF PAUL GROGAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/01/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No account statement verifying the 03/06/2020 cash balance is on file. 2) Was notice given to the VA? 3) Is the zip code for Patricia Giampa correct? 4) Paragraphs 5 & 10 of the petition refer to the "Minors" and "guardianship". How are they relevant to this case? 5) Paragraph 7(A)(f) alleges that the conservatee died testate, but according to the order approving the final account, the conservatee died intestate. 6) Paragraph 10 needs to include statements required by CRC 7.703(e)(4)&(5) for services of the paralegals. 7) The small estate affidavit of Mary Grogan appears to have been notarized prior to the conservatee's death. 8) Did the petitioner have authority to pay expenses listed as "Funeral & Burial Expenses", "Bank Fees & Charges", and "Miscellaneous" on Schedule C after the conservatee's death under Probate Code § 2631(a)? 9) Receipts signed by the beneficiaries or copies of cancelled checks showing the distributions reported on Schedule C-1 must be filed. Also, the amount of total disbursements is slightly different from the amount set forth in the order approving the final account.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF CONNIE M. MARATAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. please review the 6th amended general order for information regarding remote appearances at https://www.sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate ISSUES (to be addressed at hearing): 1) Attorney is requesting unusually high fees of $41,000.00 for 10 month accounting and includes a $5,000.00 retainer. 2) Court limits non professional fiduciary $45.00 per hour. Petitioner is requesting $75.00 per hour and appears to spend unusually high amounts of time preparing checks. 3) Examiner is unable to determine if all purchases that were reimbursed were for the conservatee. Some receipts are unreadable and have no information other than numbers. Many receipts have multiple meals which appears to be for more than 1 person. Schedule G lists liabilities for purchases of Florence Seeback of $1,460.74 and $471.83 for Ted Maratas.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CONNIE MARY MARATAS TRUST 1997; AND THE CONNIE M. MARATAS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST 2014
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Appointing Florence Seebach As Successor Trustee, Bringing Trusts Under Continuing Court Supervision, And Setting Trustee'S Bond

(LRSF 2.7, 14.6). 2) Petitioner has not filed required notice of the amended petition. Matter continued to allow time for proper notice. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends. (CRC 7.53). 3) Examiner unable to determine if an updated proposed order is necessary with the amended petition as no required courtesy copy of the petition has been received. A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WES LEE WINTERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/8/20. Notice may not be mailed in care of another unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed in writing that notice be sent in care of the second person. [CRC 7.51(a)(2).] Notice was mailed to Elizabeth C. Fitzgerald in c/o Robert Adam Leonard without any explanation. The proof of service filed on 6/25/20 now reflects the opposite of what is stated in the amended petition regarding the former and current address of Gregory Innella. (The proofs of service filed on 6/9/20 and 6/16/20 were defective, as both showed that notice was mailed on those respective dates, prior to the filing of the amended petition on 6/18/20. [LRSF 14.21 A.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TUAN VAN TRAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/15/20. No proposed order. [LRSF 14.6 A.] Notice to a minor must be given directly to a minor and also to the person having custody of the minor with whom the minor lives. [CRC 7.51(d).] If assets will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice must be given to the consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the U.S. [Probate Code Section 8113.] A supplement to the petition, signed by both petitioners and verified by at least one of them, must be filed to set forth who has custody of the minor daughter. The proposed order and courtesy copies of the supplement and the proof of service of notice to the minor as well as to the person having custody of the minor and the diplomatic or consular official of Vietnam must be received by the reviewing Examiner no later than 9/8/20.
